revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''谷中七福神'''（やなかちふくじん）とは、[[東京都]][[台東区]]・[[荒川区]]・[[北区_(東京都)|北区]]の７[[寺院]]に祀られ...'
revision.text:
'''谷中七福神'''（やなかちふくじん）とは、[[東京都]][[台東区]]・[[荒川区]]・[[北区_(東京都)|北区]]の７[[寺院]]に祀られている[[七福神]]。各お寺で[[御朱印]]押印用の版画台紙が巡礼期間に購入できる。
==七福神の構成== *[[寛永寺#弁財天|不忍池]]…[[弁財天]]。 *[[寛永寺#護国院|護国院]]…[[大黒天]]。 *[[天王寺(台東区)|天王寺]]…[[毘沙門天]]。 *[[長安寺(台東区)|長安寺]]…[[寿老人]]。 *[[修性院(荒川区)|修性院]]…[[布袋尊]]。 *[[青雲寺(荒川区)|青雲寺]]…[[恵比寿]]。 *[[東覚寺(東京都北区)|東覚寺]]…[[福禄寿]]。
==関連項目== *[[七福神]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kotsu.metro.tokyo.jp/newsevent/magazine/fureai/200501/furemado.pdf 谷中七福神]
{{DEFAULTSORT:やなかしちふくしん}} {{Japan-culture-stub}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:民間信仰]] [[Category:名数|7 しちふくしん]] [[Category:縁起物]] [[category:神仏習合]] [[Category:台東区]] [[Category:荒川区]] [[Category:東京都北区]]

'''谷中七福神'''（やなかちふくじん）は、[[東京都]][[台東区]]・[[荒川区]]・[[北区_(東京都)|北区]]の７[[寺院]]に祀られている[[七福神]]。各寺院で[[御朱印]]押印用の版画台紙が巡礼期間に購入できる。
==七福神の所在== *[[寛永寺]]（[[不忍池]]弁天堂） - [[弁才天]] *[[護国院 (東京都台東区)|護国院]] - [[大黒天]] *[[天王寺(台東区)|天王寺]] - [[毘沙門天]] *[[長安寺(台東区)|長安寺]] - [[寿老人]] *[[修性院(荒川区)|修性院]] - [[布袋]] *[[青雲寺(荒川区)|青雲寺]] - [[恵比須]] *[[東覚寺(東京都北区)|東覚寺]]…[[福禄寿]]
==関連項目== *[[七福神]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kotsu.metro.tokyo.jp/newsevent/magazine/fureai/200501/furemado.pdf 谷中七福神]
{{DEFAULTSORT:やなかしちふくしん}} {{Japan-culture-stub}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:民間信仰]] [[Category:名数|7 しちふくしん]] [[Category:縁起物]] [[category:神仏習合]] [[Category:台東区]] [[Category:荒川区]] [[Category:東京都北区]]

{{DEFAULTSORT:やなかしちふくしん}} {{Japan-culture-stub}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:民間信仰]] [[Category:名数|7 しちふくしん]] [[Category:縁起物]] [[category:神仏習合]] [[Category:台東区]] [[Category:荒川区]] [[Category:北区_(東京都)]]

'''谷中七福神'''（やなかちふくじん）は、[[東京都]][[台東区]]・[[荒川区]]・[[北区_(東京都)|北区]]の７[[寺院]]に祀られている[[七福神]]の巡礼札所。
およそ250年前に始まったと言われる江戸最古の七福神。他の七福神には神社も入っているが、谷中七福神は寺院だけである。
各寺院で[[御朱印]]押印用の[[版画]]台紙が巡礼期間に購入できる。
== 概要 == *エリア：[[東京都]][[台東区]]・[[荒川区]]・[[北区_(東京都)|北区]] *巡礼距離：約5.5km *巡礼時間：約2時間 *期間：1月1日～1月15日／9:00～17:00 *御朱印：台紙・色紙が200円、集印帳(朱印帳)は300円。
==七福神の所在== *[[寛永寺]]（[[不忍池]][[弁天堂 (寛永寺)|弁天堂]]） - [[弁才天]] *[[護国院 (寛永寺)|護国院]] - [[大黒天]] *[[天王寺 (台東区)|天王寺]] - [[毘沙門天]] *[[長安寺 (台東区)|長安寺]] - [[寿老人]] *[[修性院 (荒川区)|修性院]] - [[布袋]] *[[青雲寺 (荒川区)|青雲寺]] - [[恵比須]] *[[東覚寺 (東京都北区)|東覚寺]] - [[福禄寿]]
==隣接七福神== *[[小石川七福神]] *[[下谷七福神]]
==関連項目== *[[七福神]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kotsu.metro.tokyo.jp/newsevent/magazine/fureai/200501/furemado.pdf 谷中七福神]

revision.comment: 上位カテゴリとの重複
revision.text:
'''谷中七福神'''（やなかちふくじん）は、[[東京都]][[台東区]]・[[荒川区]]・[[北区_(東京都)|北区]]の７[[寺院]]に祀られている[[七福神]]の巡礼札所。
およそ250年前に始まったと言われる江戸最古の七福神。他の七福神には神社も入っているが、谷中七福神は寺院だけである。
各寺院で[[御朱印]]押印用の[[版画]]台紙が巡礼期間に購入できる。
== 総論 == *エリア：[[東京都]][[台東区]]・[[荒川区]]・[[北区_(東京都)|北区]] *巡礼距離：約5.5km *巡礼時間：約2時間 *期間：1月1日～1月15日／9:00～17:00 *御朱印：台紙・色紙が200円、集印帳(朱印帳)は300円。
==七福神の所在== *[[寛永寺]]（[[不忍池]][[弁天堂 (寛永寺)|弁天堂]]） - [[弁才天]] *[[護国院 (台東区)|護国院]] - [[大黒天]] *[[天王寺 (台東区)|天王寺]] - [[毘沙門天]] *[[長安寺 (台東区)|長安寺]] - [[寿老人]] *[[修性院 (荒川区)|修性院]] - [[布袋]] *[[青雲寺 (荒川区)|青雲寺]] - [[恵比須]] *[[東覚寺 (東京都北区)|東覚寺]] - [[福禄寿]]
==隣接七福神== *[[小石川七福神]] *[[下谷七福神]]
==関連項目== *[[七福神]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kotsu.metro.tokyo.jp/newsevent/magazine/fureai/200501/furemado.pdf 谷中七福神]
{{DEFAULTSORT:やなかしちふくしん}} [[Category:七福神]] [[Category:台東区]] [[Category:荒川区]] [[Category:北区_(東京都)]] {{Japan-culture-stub}}

'''谷中七福神'''（やなかしちふくじん）は、[[東京都]][[台東区]]・[[荒川区]]・[[北区 (東京都)|北区]]の7[[寺院]]に祀られている[[七福神]]の巡礼札所。
およそ250年前に始まったと言われる江戸最古の七福神。他の七福神には神社も入っているが、谷中七福神は寺院だけである。
各寺院で[[朱印 (神社仏閣)|朱印]]押印用の[[版画]]台紙が巡礼期間に購入できる。 期間外は、境内に入れても板戸が閉まり本堂には入れず七福神が見えない事もある。
== 総論 == *エリア：[[東京都]][[台東区]]・[[荒川区]]・[[北区 (東京都)|北区]] *巡礼距離：約5.5km *巡礼時間：約2時間 *期間：1月1日 - 1月10日（平成21年まで1月1日 - 1月15日）／9:00 - 17:00 *御朱印：台紙・色紙が200円、集印帳（朱印帳）は300円。
== 谷中七福神の一覧 == *[[寛永寺]] [[不忍池]][[寛永寺#現存する伽藍|弁天堂]] - [[弁才天]] *[[護国院 (台東区)|護国院]]（寛永寺子院） - [[大黒天]] *[[天王寺 (台東区)|天王寺]] - [[毘沙門天]] *[[長安寺 (台東区)|長安寺]] - [[寿老人]] *[[修性院 (荒川区)|修性院]] - [[布袋]] *[[青雲寺 (荒川区)|青雲寺]] - [[恵比須]] *[[東覚寺 (東京都北区)|東覚寺]] - [[福禄寿]] *徒歩での巡拝の場合、啓運寺の前を通り、啓運寺に毘沙門天が安置されているが、当寺は谷中七福神とは関係はない。 *谷中七福神は今の組み合わせが、江戸時代から限られていたわけではなく、恵比寿は神田明神や清水観音堂、大黒天は経王寺、毘沙門天は谷中本通寺、弁財天は北町の[[養泉寺 (台東区)|養泉寺]]とする事もあった。
== 隣接七福神 == *[[小石川七福神]] *[[下谷七福神]]
== 関連項目 == *[[七福神]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://taitonavi.jp/pdf/pamph_ja/044.pdf 台東ぶらり散歩 其の五【七福神めぐり】]

'''谷中七福神'''（やなかしちふくじん）は、[[東京都]][[台東区]]・[[荒川区]]・[[北区 (東京都)|北区]]の7[[寺院]]に祀られている[[七福神]]の巡礼札所。
およそ250年前に始まったと言われる江戸最古の七福神。他の七福神には神社も入っているが、谷中七福神は寺院だけである。
各寺院で[[朱印 (神社仏閣)|朱印]]押印用の[[版画]]台紙が巡礼期間に購入できる。 期間外は、境内に入れても板戸が閉まり本堂には入れず七福神が見えない事もある。
== 総論 == *エリア：[[東京都]][[台東区]]・[[荒川区]]・[[北区 (東京都)|北区]] *巡礼距離：約5.5km *巡礼時間：約2時間 *期間：1月1日 - 1月10日（平成21年まで1月1日 - 1月15日）／9:00 - 17:00 *御朱印：台紙・色紙が200円、集印帳（朱印帳）は300円。 **御朱印台紙：台紙は1000円。谷中七福神では厚紙の色紙ではなく、和紙で出来た台紙にいただく（A3ほどの大きさの規格外サイズ）。 **近年、谷中七福神の御朱印代が500円の寺院もある（現在300円の寺院も500円に今後改定される場合があり）。 **公式に発表されていないが、白紙の厚紙色紙を持参した場合、そこに各七福神の御朱印をいただくことができる（要問合せ）。
== 谷中七福神の一覧 == *[[寛永寺]] [[不忍池]][[寛永寺#現存する伽藍|弁天堂]] - [[弁才天]] *[[護国院 (台東区)|護国院]]（寛永寺子院） - [[大黒天]] *[[天王寺 (台東区)|天王寺]] - [[毘沙門天]] *[[長安寺 (台東区)|長安寺]] - [[寿老人]] *[[修性院 (荒川区)|修性院]] - [[布袋]] *[[青雲寺 (荒川区)|青雲寺]] - [[恵比須]] *[[東覚寺 (東京都北区)|東覚寺]] - [[福禄寿]] *谷中七福神付近の[[修性院 (荒川区)|修性院]]付近に[[啓運寺 (荒川区)|啓運寺]]があり、当寺に毘沙門天が安置されている（徒歩での巡拝の場合、啓運寺の前を通る場合が多い）。しかし当寺は谷中七福神とは関係はない。 *谷中七福神は今の組み合わせが、江戸時代から限られていたわけではなく、恵比寿は神田明神や清水観音堂、大黒天は経王寺、毘沙門天は谷中本通寺、弁財天は北町の[[養泉寺 (台東区)|養泉寺]]とする事もあった。
== 隣接七福神 == *[[小石川七福神]] *[[下谷七福神]]
== 関連項目 == *[[七福神]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://taitonavi.jp/pdf/pamph_ja/044.pdf 台東ぶらり散歩 其の五【七福神めぐり】]

各寺院で[[朱印 (神社仏閣)|朱印]]押印用の[[版画]]台紙が巡礼期間に購入できる。 期間外は、境内に入れても板戸が閉まり本堂には入れず七福神が見えない事もある。

== 谷中七福神の一覧 == *[[寛永寺]] [[不忍池]][[寛永寺#現存する伽藍|弁天堂]] - [[弁才天]]([[台東区]][[上野桜木]]) *[[護国院 (台東区)|護国院]]（寛永寺子院） - [[大黒天]](台東区[[上野恩賜公園]]) *[[天王寺 (台東区)|天王寺]] - [[毘沙門天]](台東区[[谷中 (台東区)|谷中]]) *[[長安寺 (台東区)|長安寺]] - [[寿老人]](台東区谷中) *[[修性院]] - [[布袋]]([[荒川区]][[西日暮里]]) *[[青雲寺 (荒川区)|青雲寺]] - [[恵比須]](荒川区西日暮里) *[[東覚寺 (東京都北区)|東覚寺]] - [[福禄寿]]([[北区 (東京都)|北区]][[田端 (東京都北区)|田端]]) *谷中七福神の修性院付近に[[啓運寺 (荒川区)|啓運寺]]があり、当寺に毘沙門天が安置されている（徒歩での巡拝の場合、啓運寺の前を通る場合が多い）。しかし当寺は谷中七福神とは関係はない。 *谷中七福神は今の組み合わせが、江戸時代から限られていたわけではなく、恵比寿は神田明神や清水観音堂、大黒天は経王寺、毘沙門天は谷中本通寺、弁財天は北町の[[養泉寺 (台東区)|養泉寺]]とする事もあった。
== 隣接七福神 == *[[小石川七福神]] *[[下谷七福神]]
== 関連項目 == *[[七福神]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://taitonavi.jp/pdf/pamph_ja/044.pdf 台東ぶらり散歩 其の五【七福神めぐり】]

{{Infobox Buddhist pilgrimage | Name = Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods | Image = | Location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taitō|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Arakawa, Tokyo|ja|荒川区}}, {{ill|Kita, Tokyo|ja|北区}} | Distance = Approx. 5.5 km | Time = Approx. 2 hours | Period = January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 | Stamps = Paper or colored paper ¥200, Goshuin book ¥300 | Stamp Sheet = Paper sheet ¥1,000, unique to Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods (A3-sized Japanese paper) | Notes = Some temples have raised goshuin fees to ¥500 (as of recent years) }}
'''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (谷中七福神) is a **pilgrimage route** of seven **Buddhist temples** in **Taitō, Arakawa, and Kita Wards, Tokyo**, each enshrining one of the {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}.
It is believed to have started **about 250 years ago**, making it the **oldest Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimage in Edo (Tokyo)**. Unlike other Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimages, which include **Shinto shrines**, the **Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods consist only of Buddhist temples**.
During the pilgrimage period, visitors can purchase **woodblock-printed stamp sheets** to collect {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}} (temple stamps). Outside this period, although temple grounds are generally accessible, the **Seven Lucky Gods statues may not be visible**, as **halls may be closed**.
== Overview ==
* **Area:** {{ill|Taitō, Tokyo|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Arakawa, Tokyo|ja|荒川区}}, {{ill|Kita, Tokyo|ja|北区}} * **Distance:** Approx. 5.5 km * **Time required:** Approx. 2 hours * **Pilgrimage period:** January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 * **Temple stamps (Goshuin):** * **Paper or colored paper:** ¥200 * **Goshuin book (shuinchō):** ¥300 * **Stamp sheet:** ¥1,000, made of **Japanese paper (not thick colored paper)**, unique to **Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods** (approx. **A3 size**). * In **recent years**, some temples have increased the goshuin fee to **¥500** (others may follow suit). * Although **not officially announced**, **if visitors bring a blank thick-paper sheet**, it is possible to collect **goshuin** on it (inquire at each temple).
== Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods List ==
* **{{ill|Kaneiji|ja|寛永寺}}** – **Benzaiten** (弁才天) at **Benten-dō** (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区}} * **{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院}}** (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – **Daikokuten** (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区}} * **{{ill|Tennōji (Taitō)|ja|天王寺 (台東区)}}** – **Bishamonten** (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区}} * **{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)}}** – **Jurojin** (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * **{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院}}** – **Hotei** (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区}} * **{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)}}** – **Ebisu** (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * **{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)}}** – **Fukurokuju** (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区}}
* Near **Shōjōin**, there is **{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺}}**, which enshrines **Bishamonten**. Many pilgrims **pass by this temple**, but it is **not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route**. * The composition of the **Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods** was **not fixed during the Edo period**. In the past: * **Ebisu** was enshrined at **Kanda Myōjin** or **Shimizu Kannon-dō**. * **Daikokuten** was at **Kyōōji**. * **Bishamonten** was at **Hon-dōji in Yanaka**. * **Benzaiten** was at **Yōsenji in Kitamachi**.
== Nearby Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimages ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Seven Lucky Gods|ja|小石川七福神}} * {{ill|Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|ja|下谷七福神}}
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}
== External Links ==
* [http://taitonavi.jp/pdf/pamph_ja/044.pdf Taitō Strolling Guide: Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage]

{{Infobox Buddhist pilgrimage | Name = Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods | Image = | Location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taitō|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Arakawa, Tokyo|ja|荒川区}}, {{ill|Kita, Tokyo|ja|北区}} | Distance = Approx. 5.5 km | Time = Approx. 2 hours | Period = January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 | Stamps = Paper or colored paper ¥200, Goshuin book ¥300 | Stamp Sheet = Paper sheet ¥1,000, unique to Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods (A3-sized Japanese paper) | Notes = Some temples have raised goshuin fees to ¥500 (as of recent years) }}

== Overview ==
* **Area:** {{ill|Taitō, Tokyo|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Arakawa, Tokyo|ja|荒川区}}, {{ill|Kita, Tokyo|ja|北区}} * **Distance:** Approx. 5.5 km * **Time required:** Approx. 2 hours * **Pilgrimage period:** January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 * **Temple stamps (Goshuin):** * **Paper or colored paper:** ¥200 * **Goshuin book (shuinchō):** ¥300 * **Stamp sheet:** ¥1,000, made of **Japanese paper (not thick colored paper)**, unique to **Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods** (approx. **A3 size**). * In **recent years**, some temples have increased the goshuin fee to **¥500** (others may follow suit). * Although **not officially announced**, **if visitors bring a blank thick-paper sheet**, it is possible to collect **goshuin** on it (inquire at each temple).
== Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods List ==
* **{{ill|Kaneiji|ja|寛永寺}}** – **Benzaiten** (弁才天) at **Benten-dō** (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区}} * **{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院}}** (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – **Daikokuten** (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区}} * **{{ill|Tennōji (Taitō)|ja|天王寺 (台東区)}}** – **Bishamonten** (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区}} * **{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)}}** – **Jurojin** (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * **{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院}}** – **Hotei** (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区}} * **{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)}}** – **Ebisu** (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * **{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)}}** – **Fukurokuju** (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区}}
* Near **Shōjōin**, there is **{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺}}**, which enshrines **Bishamonten**. Many pilgrims **pass by this temple**, but it is **not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route**. * The composition of the **Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods** was **not fixed during the Edo period**. In the past: * **Ebisu** was enshrined at **Kanda Myōjin** or **Shimizu Kannon-dō**. * **Daikokuten** was at **Kyōōji**. * **Bishamonten** was at **Hon-dōji in Yanaka**. * **Benzaiten** was at **Yōsenji in Kitamachi**.

* Near **Shōjōin**, there is **{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺}}**, which enshrines **Bishamonten**. In the past: * **Ebisu** was enshrined at **Kanda Myōjin** or **Shimizu Kannon-dō**. * **Daikokuten** was at **Kyōōji**. * **Bishamonten** was at **Hon-dōji in Yanaka**. * **Benzaiten** was at **Yōsenji in Kitamachi**.

'''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (谷中七福神) is a **pilgrimage route** of seven **Buddhist temples** in **Taitō, Arakawa, and Kita Wards, Tokyo**, each enshrining one of the {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}.
It is believed to have started **about 250 years ago**, making it the **oldest Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimage in Edo (Tokyo)**. Unlike other Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimages, which include **Shinto shrines**, the **Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods consist only of Buddhist temples**.
During the pilgrimage period, visitors can purchase **woodblock-printed stamp sheets** to collect {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}} (temple stamps). Outside this period, although temple grounds are generally accessible, the **Seven Lucky Gods statues may not be visible**, as **halls may be closed**.
== Overview ==
* **Area:** {{ill|Taitō, Tokyo|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Arakawa, Tokyo|ja|荒川区}}, {{ill|Kita, Tokyo|ja|北区}} * **Distance:** Approx. 5.5 km * **Time required:** Approx. 2 hours * **Pilgrimage period:** January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 * **Temple stamps (Goshuin):** * **Paper or colored paper:** ¥200 * **Goshuin book (shuinchō):** ¥300 * **Stamp sheet:** ¥1,000, made of **Japanese paper (not thick colored paper)**, unique to **Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods** (approx. **A3 size**). * In **recent years**, some temples have increased the goshuin fee to **¥500** (others may follow suit). * Although **not officially announced**, **if visitors bring a blank thick-paper sheet**, it is possible to collect **goshuin** on it (inquire at each temple).
== Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods List ==
* **{{ill|Kaneiji|ja|寛永寺}}** – **Benzaiten** (弁才天) at **Benten-dō** (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区}} * **{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院}}** (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – **Daikokuten** (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区}} * **{{ill|Tennōji (Taitō)|ja|天王寺 (台東区)}}** – **Bishamonten** (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区}} * **{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)}}** – **Jurojin** (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * **{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院}}** – **Hotei** (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区}} * **{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)}}** – **Ebisu** (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * **{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)}}** – **Fukurokuju** (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区}}
* Near **Shōjōin**, there is **{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺}}**, which enshrines **Bishamonten**. Many pilgrims **pass by this temple**, but it is **not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route**. * The composition of the **Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods** was **not fixed during the Edo period**. In the past: * **Ebisu** was enshrined at **Kanda Myōjin** or **Shimizu Kannon-dō**. * **Daikokuten** was at **Kyōōji**. * **Bishamonten** was at **Hon-dōji in Yanaka**. * **Benzaiten** was at **Yōsenji in Kitamachi**.
== Nearby Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimages ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Seven Lucky Gods|ja|小石川七福神}} * {{ill|Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|ja|下谷七福神}}
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}
== External Links ==

[[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:谷中七福神]] {{Japan-culture-stub}}[[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]][[ja:Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods]]

{{Infobox Buddhist pilgrimage | Name = Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods | Image = | Location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taitō|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Arakawa, Tokyo|ja|荒川区}}, {{ill|Kita, Tokyo|ja|北区}} | Distance = Approx. 5.5 km | Time = Approx. 2 hours | Period = January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 | Stamps = Paper or colored paper ¥200, Goshuin book ¥300 | Stamp Sheet = Paper sheet ¥1,000, unique to Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods (A3-sized Japanese paper) | Notes = Some temples have raised goshuin fees to ¥500 (as of recent years) }}
'''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (谷中七福神) is a '''pilgrimage route''' of seven '''Buddhist temples''' in '''Taitō, Arakawa, and Kita Wards, Tokyo''', each enshrining one of the {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}.
It is believed to have started '''about 250 years ago''', making it the '''oldest Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimage in Edo (Tokyo)'''. Unlike other Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimages, which include '''Shinto shrines''', the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods consist only of Buddhist temples'''.
During the pilgrimage period, visitors can purchase '''woodblock-printed stamp sheets''' to collect {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}} (temple stamps). Outside this period, although temple grounds are generally accessible, the '''Seven Lucky Gods statues may not be visible''', as '''halls may be closed'''.
== Overview ==
* '''Area:''' {{ill|Taitō, Tokyo|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Arakawa, Tokyo|ja|荒川区}}, {{ill|Kita, Tokyo|ja|北区}} * '''Distance:''' Approx. 5.5 km * '''Time required:''' Approx. 2 hours * '''Pilgrimage period:''' January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 * '''Temple stamps (Goshuin):''' * '''Paper or colored paper:''' ¥200 * '''Goshuin book (shuinchō):''' ¥300 * '''Stamp sheet:''' ¥1,000, made of '''Japanese paper (not thick colored paper)''', unique to '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (approx. '''A3 size'''). * In '''recent years''', some temples have increased the goshuin fee to '''¥500''' (others may follow suit). * Although '''not officially announced''', '''if visitors bring a blank thick-paper sheet''', it is possible to collect '''goshuin''' on it (inquire at each temple).
== Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods List ==
* '''{{ill|Kaneiji|ja|寛永寺}}''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区}} * '''{{ill|Tennōji (Taitō)|ja|天王寺 (台東区)}}''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区}}
* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: * '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. * '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. * '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. * '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.
== Nearby Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimages ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Seven Lucky Gods|ja|小石川七福神}} * {{ill|Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|ja|下谷七福神}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}
== External Links ==
* [http://taitonavi.jp/pdf/pamph_ja/044.pdf Taitō Strolling Guide: Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage]

* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: * '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. * '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. * '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. * '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.
== Nearby Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimages ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Seven Lucky Gods|ja|小石川七福神}} * {{ill|Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|ja|下谷七福神}}

* {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}

{{Infobox Buddhist pilgrimage | Name = Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods | Image = | Location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taitō|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Arakawa, Tokyo|ja|荒川区}}, {{ill|Kita, Tokyo|ja|北区}} | Distance = Approx. 5.5 km | Time = Approx. 2 hours | Period = January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 | Stamps = Paper or colored paper ¥200, Goshuin book ¥300 | Stamp Sheet = Paper sheet ¥1,000, unique to Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods (A3-sized Japanese paper) | Notes = Some temples have raised goshuin fees to ¥500 (as of recent years) }}
'''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (谷中七福神) is a '''pilgrimage route''' of seven '''Buddhist temples''' in '''Taitō, Arakawa, and Kita Wards, Tokyo''', each enshrining one of the {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}.
It is believed to have started '''about 250 years ago''', making it the '''oldest Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimage in Edo (Tokyo)'''. Unlike other Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimages, which include '''Shinto shrines''', the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods consist only of Buddhist temples'''.
During the pilgrimage period, visitors can purchase '''woodblock-printed stamp sheets''' to collect {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}} (temple stamps). Outside this period, although temple grounds are generally accessible, the '''Seven Lucky Gods statues may not be visible''', as '''halls may be closed'''.
== Overview ==
* '''Area:''' {{ill|Taitō, Tokyo|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Arakawa, Tokyo|ja|荒川区}}, {{ill|Kita, Tokyo|ja|北区}} * '''Distance:''' Approx. 5.5 km * '''Time required:''' Approx. 2 hours * '''Pilgrimage period:''' January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 * '''Temple stamps (Goshuin):''' * '''Paper or colored paper:''' ¥200 * '''Goshuin book (shuinchō):''' ¥300 * '''Stamp sheet:''' ¥1,000, made of '''Japanese paper (not thick colored paper)''', unique to '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (approx. '''A3 size'''). * In '''recent years''', some temples have increased the goshuin fee to '''¥500''' (others may follow suit). * Although '''not officially announced''', '''if visitors bring a blank thick-paper sheet''', it is possible to collect '''goshuin''' on it (inquire at each temple).
== Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods List ==
* '''{{ill|Kaneiji|ja|寛永寺}}''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区}} * '''{{ill|Tennōji (Taitō)|ja|天王寺 (台東区)}}''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区}}
* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: * '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. * '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. * '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. * '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.
== Nearby Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimages ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Seven Lucky Gods|ja|小石川七福神}} * {{ill|Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|ja|下谷七福神}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}

'''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (谷中七福神) is a '''pilgrimage route''' of seven '''Buddhist temples''' in '''Taitō, Arakawa, and Kita Wards, Tokyo''', each enshrining one of the {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}.
It is believed to have started '''about 250 years ago''', making it the '''oldest Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimage in Edo (Tokyo)'''. Unlike other Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimages, which include '''Shinto shrines''', the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods consist only of Buddhist temples'''.
During the pilgrimage period, visitors can purchase '''woodblock-printed stamp sheets''' to collect {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}} (temple stamps). Outside this period, although temple grounds are generally accessible, the '''Seven Lucky Gods statues may not be visible''', as '''halls may be closed'''.
== Overview ==
* '''Area:''' {{ill|Taitō, Tokyo|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Arakawa, Tokyo|ja|荒川区}}, {{ill|Kita, Tokyo|ja|北区}} * '''Distance:''' Approx. 5.5 km * '''Time required:''' Approx. 2 hours * '''Pilgrimage period:''' January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 * '''Temple stamps (Goshuin):''' * '''Paper or colored paper:''' ¥200 * '''Goshuin book (shuinchō):''' ¥300 * '''Stamp sheet:''' ¥1,000, made of '''Japanese paper (not thick colored paper)''', unique to '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (approx. '''A3 size'''). * In '''recent years''', some temples have increased the goshuin fee to '''¥500''' (others may follow suit). * Although '''not officially announced''', '''if visitors bring a blank thick-paper sheet''', it is possible to collect '''goshuin''' on it (inquire at each temple).
== Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods List ==
* '''{{ill|Kaneiji|ja|寛永寺}}''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区}} * '''{{ill|Tennōji (Taitō)|ja|天王寺 (台東区)}}''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区}}
* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: * '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. * '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. * '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. * '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.
== Nearby Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimages ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Seven Lucky Gods|ja|小石川七福神}} * {{ill|Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|ja|下谷七福神}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}
== External Links ==
* [http://taitonavi.jp/pdf/pamph_ja/044.pdf Taitō Strolling Guide: Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage]

* '''{{ill|Kaneiji|ja|寛永寺}}''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区}} * '''{{ill|Tennōji (Taitō)|ja|天王寺 (台東区)}}''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区}}
* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: ** '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. ** '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. ** '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. ** '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.

'''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (谷中七福神) is a '''pilgrimage route''' of seven '''Buddhist temples''' in '''Taitō, Arakawa, and Kita Wards, Tokyo''', each enshrining one of the {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}.
It is believed to have started '''about 250 years ago''', making it the '''oldest Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimage in Edo (Tokyo)'''. Unlike other Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimages, which include '''Shinto shrines''', the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods consist only of Buddhist temples'''.
During the pilgrimage period, visitors can purchase '''woodblock-printed stamp sheets''' to collect {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}} (temple stamps). Outside this period, although temple grounds are generally accessible, the '''Seven Lucky Gods statues may not be visible''', as '''halls may be closed'''.
== Overview ==
* '''Area:''' {{ill|Taitō, Tokyo|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Arakawa, Tokyo|ja|荒川区}}, {{ill|Kita, Tokyo|ja|北区}} * '''Distance:''' Approx. 5.5 km * '''Time required:''' Approx. 2 hours * '''Pilgrimage period:''' January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 * '''Temple stamps (Goshuin):''' ** '''Paper or colored paper:''' ¥200 ** '''Goshuin book (shuinchō):''' ¥300 ** '''Stamp sheet:''' ¥1,000, made of '''Japanese paper (not thick colored paper)''', unique to '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (approx. '''A3 size'''). ** In '''recent years''', some temples have increased the goshuin fee to '''¥500''' (others may follow suit). ** Although '''not officially announced''', '''if visitors bring a blank thick-paper sheet''', it is possible to collect '''goshuin''' on it (inquire at each temple).
== Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods List ==
* '''{{ill|Kaneiji|ja|寛永寺}}''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区}} * '''{{ill|Tennōji (Taitō)|ja|天王寺 (台東区)}}''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区}}
* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: ** '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. ** '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. ** '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. ** '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.

{{Infobox Buddhist pilgrimage | Name = Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods | image = | Location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taitō|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Arakawa, Tokyo|ja|荒川区}}, {{ill|Kita, Tokyo|ja|北区}} | Distance = Approx. 5.5 km | Time = Approx. 2 hours | Period = January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 | Stamps = Paper or colored paper ¥200, Goshuin book ¥300 | Stamp Sheet = Paper sheet ¥1,000, unique to Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods (A3-sized Japanese paper) | Notes = Some temples have raised goshuin fees to ¥500 (as of recent years) }}
'''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (谷中七福神) is a '''pilgrimage route''' of seven '''Buddhist temples''' in '''Taitō, Arakawa, and Kita Wards, Tokyo''', each enshrining one of the {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}.
It is believed to have started '''about 250 years ago''', making it the '''oldest Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimage in Edo (Tokyo)'''. Unlike other Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimages, which include '''Shinto shrines''', the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods consist only of Buddhist temples'''.
During the pilgrimage period, visitors can purchase '''woodblock-printed stamp sheets''' to collect {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}} (temple stamps). Outside this period, although temple grounds are generally accessible, the '''Seven Lucky Gods statues may not be visible''', as '''halls may be closed'''.
== Overview ==
* '''Area:''' {{ill|Taitō, Tokyo|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Arakawa, Tokyo|ja|荒川区}}, {{ill|Kita, Tokyo|ja|北区}} * '''Distance:''' Approx. 5.5 km * '''Time required:''' Approx. 2 hours * '''Pilgrimage period:''' January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 * '''Temple stamps (Goshuin):''' ** '''Paper or colored paper:''' ¥200 ** '''Goshuin book (shuinchō):''' ¥300 ** '''Stamp sheet:''' ¥1,000, made of '''Japanese paper (not thick colored paper)''', unique to '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (approx. '''A3 size'''). ** In '''recent years''', some temples have increased the goshuin fee to '''¥500''' (others may follow suit). ** Although '''not officially announced''', '''if visitors bring a blank thick-paper sheet''', it is possible to collect '''goshuin''' on it (inquire at each temple).
== Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods List ==
* '''{{ill|Kaneiji|ja|寛永寺}}''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区}} * '''{{ill|Tennōji (Taitō)|ja|天王寺 (台東区)}}''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区}}
* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: ** '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. ** '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. ** '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. ** '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.
== Nearby Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimages ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Seven Lucky Gods|ja|小石川七福神}} * {{ill|Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|ja|下谷七福神}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}
== External Links ==

== Overview ==
* '''Area:''' {{ill|Taitō, Tokyo|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Arakawa, Tokyo|ja|荒川区}}, {{ill|Kita, Tokyo|ja|北区}} * '''Distance:''' Approx. 5.5 km * '''Time required:''' Approx. 2 hours * '''Pilgrimage period:''' January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 * '''Temple stamps (Goshuin):''' ** '''Paper or colored paper:''' ¥200 ** '''Goshuin book (shuinchō):''' ¥300 ** '''Stamp sheet:''' ¥1,000, made of '''Japanese paper (not thick colored paper)''', unique to '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (approx. '''A3 size'''). ** In '''recent years''', some temples have increased the goshuin fee to '''¥500''' (others may follow suit). ** Although '''not officially announced''', '''if visitors bring a blank thick-paper sheet''', it is possible to collect '''goshuin''' on it (inquire at each temple).
== Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods List ==
* '''{{ill|Kaneiji|ja|寛永寺}}''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区}} * '''{{ill|Tennōji (Taitō)|ja|天王寺 (台東区)}}''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区}}
* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: ** '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. ** '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. ** '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. ** '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.
== Nearby Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimages ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Seven Lucky Gods|ja|小石川七福神}} * {{ill|Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|ja|下谷七福神}} == See Also ==
* {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}

{{Infobox Buddhist pilgrimage | Name = Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods | image = | Location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taitō|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Arakawa, Tokyo|ja|荒川区}}, {{ill|Kita, Tokyo|ja|北区}} | Distance = Approx. 5.5 km | Time = Approx. 2 hours | Period = January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 | Stamps = Paper or colored paper ¥200, Goshuin book ¥300 | Stamp Sheet = Paper sheet ¥1,000, unique to Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods (A3-sized Japanese paper) | Notes = Some temples have raised goshuin fees to ¥500 (as of recent years) }}
'''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (谷中七福神) is a '''pilgrimage route''' of seven '''Buddhist temples''' in '''Taitō, Arakawa, and Kita Wards, Tokyo''', each enshrining one of the {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}.
It is believed to have started '''about 250 years ago''', making it the '''oldest Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimage in Edo (Tokyo)'''. Unlike other Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimages, which include '''Shinto shrines''', the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods consist only of Buddhist temples'''.
During the pilgrimage period, visitors can purchase '''woodblock-printed stamp sheets''' to collect {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}} (temple stamps). Outside this period, although temple grounds are generally accessible, the '''Seven Lucky Gods statues may not be visible''', as '''halls may be closed'''.
== Overview ==
* '''Area:''' {{ill|Taitō, Tokyo|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Arakawa, Tokyo|ja|荒川区}}, {{ill|Kita, Tokyo|ja|北区}} * '''Distance:''' Approx. 5.5 km * '''Time required:''' Approx. 2 hours * '''Pilgrimage period:''' January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 * '''Temple stamps (Goshuin):''' ** '''Paper or colored paper:''' ¥200 ** '''Goshuin book (shuinchō):''' ¥300 ** '''Stamp sheet:''' ¥1,000, made of '''Japanese paper (not thick colored paper)''', unique to '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (approx. '''A3 size'''). ** In '''recent years''', some temples have increased the goshuin fee to '''¥500''' (others may follow suit). ** Although '''not officially announced''', '''if visitors bring a blank thick-paper sheet''', it is possible to collect '''goshuin''' on it (inquire at each temple).
== Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods List ==
* '''{{ill|Kaneiji|ja|寛永寺}}''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区}} * '''{{ill|Tennōji (Taitō)|ja|天王寺 (台東区)}}''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区}}
* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: ** '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. ** '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. ** '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. ** '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.
== Nearby Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimages ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Seven Lucky Gods|ja|小石川七福神}} * {{ill|Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|ja|下谷七福神}} == See Also ==
* {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}
== External Links ==
* [http://taitonavi.jp/pdf/pamph_ja/044.pdf Taitō Strolling Guide: Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage]

revision.text:
'''谷中七福神'''（やなかしちふくじん）は、[[東京都]][[台東区]]・[[荒川区]]・[[北区 (東京都)|北区]]の7[[寺院]]に祀られている[[七福神]]の巡礼札所。
およそ250年前に始まったと言われる江戸最古の七福神。他の七福神には神社も入っているが、谷中七福神は寺院だけである。
各寺院で[[朱印 (神社仏閣)|朱印]]押印用の[[版画]]台紙が巡礼期間に購入できる。 期間外は、境内に入れても板戸が閉まり本堂には入れず七福神が見えない事もある。
== 総論 == *エリア：[[東京都]][[台東区]]・[[荒川区]]・[[北区 (東京都)|北区]] *巡礼距離：約5.5km *巡礼時間：約2時間 *期間：1月1日 - 1月10日（平成21年まで1月1日 - 1月15日）／9:00 - 17:00 *御朱印：台紙・色紙が200円、集印帳（朱印帳）は300円。 **御朱印台紙：台紙は1000円。谷中七福神では厚紙の色紙ではなく、和紙で出来た台紙にいただく（A3ほどの大きさの規格外サイズ）。 **近年、谷中七福神の御朱印代が500円の寺院もある（現在300円の寺院も500円に今後改定される場合があり）。 **公式に発表されていないが、白紙の厚紙色紙を持参した場合、そこに各七福神の御朱印をいただくことができる（要問合せ）。
== 谷中七福神の一覧 == *[[寛永寺]] [[不忍池]][[寛永寺#現存する伽藍|弁天堂]] - [[弁才天]]([[台東区]][[上野桜木]]) *[[護国院 (台東区)|護国院]]（寛永寺子院） - [[大黒天]](台東区[[上野恩賜公園]]) *[[天王寺 (台東区)|天王寺]] - [[毘沙門天]](台東区[[谷中 (台東区)|谷中]]) *[[長安寺 (台東区)|長安寺]] - [[寿老人]](台東区谷中) *[[修性院]] - [[布袋]]([[荒川区]][[西日暮里]]) *[[青雲寺 (荒川区)|青雲寺]] - [[恵比須]](荒川区西日暮里) *[[東覚寺 (東京都北区)|東覚寺]] - [[福禄寿]]([[北区 (東京都)|北区]][[田端 (東京都北区)|田端]]) *谷中七福神の修性院付近に[[啓運寺 (荒川区)|啓運寺]]があり、当寺に毘沙門天が安置されている（徒歩での巡拝の場合、啓運寺の前を通る場合が多い）。しかし当寺は谷中七福神とは関係はない。 *谷中七福神は今の組み合わせが、江戸時代から限られていたわけではなく、恵比寿は神田明神や清水観音堂、大黒天は経王寺、毘沙門天は谷中本通寺、弁財天は北町の[[養泉寺 (台東区)|養泉寺]]とする事もあった。
== 隣接七福神 == *[[小石川七福神]] *[[下谷七福神]]
== 関連項目 == *[[七福神]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://taitonavi.jp/pdf/pamph_ja/044.pdf 台東ぶらり散歩 其の五【七福神めぐり】]
{{DEFAULTSORT:やなかしちふくしん}} [[Category:七福神めぐり]] [[Category:台東区の歴史]] [[Category:荒川区の歴史]] [[Category:東京都北区の歴史]] [[Category:谷中]] {{Japan-culture-stub}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[谷中七福神]] to [[Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

== 隣接七福神 == *[[小石川七福神]] *[[下谷七福神]]
== 関連項目 == *[[七福神]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://taitonavi.jp/pdf/pamph_ja/044.pdf 台東ぶらり散歩 其の五【七福神めぐり】]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:谷中七福神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11632598]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist pilgrimage | Name = Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods | image = | Location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Taitō|Taitō]], [[:en:Arakawa, Tokyo|Arakawa, Tokyo]], [[:en:北區|Kita, Tokyo]] | Distance = Approx. 5.5 km | Time = Approx. 2 hours | Period = January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 | Stamps = Paper or colored paper ¥200, Goshuin book ¥300 | Stamp Sheet = Paper sheet ¥1,000, unique to Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods (A3-sized Japanese paper) | Notes = Some temples have raised goshuin fees to ¥500 (as of recent years) }}
'''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (谷中七福神) is a '''pilgrimage route''' of seven '''Buddhist temples''' in '''Taitō, Arakawa, and Kita Wards, Tokyo''', each enshrining one of the [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]].
It is believed to have started '''about 250 years ago''', making it the '''oldest Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimage in Edo (Tokyo)'''. Unlike other Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimages, which include '''Shinto shrines''', the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods consist only of Buddhist temples'''.
During the pilgrimage period, visitors can purchase '''woodblock-printed stamp sheets''' to collect {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}} (temple stamps). Outside this period, although temple grounds are generally accessible, the '''Seven Lucky Gods statues may not be visible''', as '''halls may be closed'''.
== Overview ==
* '''Area:''' [[:en:Taitō|Taitō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Arakawa, Tokyo|Arakawa, Tokyo]], [[:en:北區|Kita, Tokyo]] * '''Distance:''' Approx. 5.5 km * '''Time required:''' Approx. 2 hours * '''Pilgrimage period:''' January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 * '''Temple stamps (Goshuin):''' ** '''Paper or colored paper:''' ¥200 ** '''Goshuin book (shuinchō):''' ¥300 ** '''Stamp sheet:''' ¥1,000, made of '''Japanese paper (not thick colored paper)''', unique to '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (approx. '''A3 size'''). ** In '''recent years''', some temples have increased the goshuin fee to '''¥500''' (others may follow suit). ** Although '''not officially announced''', '''if visitors bring a blank thick-paper sheet''', it is possible to collect '''goshuin''' on it (inquire at each temple).
== Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods List ==
* '''[[:en:Kan'ei-ji|Kaneiji]]''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenosakuragi, Taitō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokokuin (Taitō)|qq=|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''[[:en:Tennō-ji (Taitō)|Tennōji (Taitō)]]''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanaka, Taitō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōanji (Taitō)|qq=|1=Chōan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106629524}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjōin (Arakawa)|qq=|1=Shusho-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97214954}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinippori, Arakawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiunji (Arakawa)|qq=|1=Seiun-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97216427}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|qq=|1=Togaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019198}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiunji (Arakawa)|qq=|1=Keiunji (Arakawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418872}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: ** '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. ** '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. ** '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. ** '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.
== Nearby Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimages ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Seven Lucky Gods|ja|小石川七福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koishikawa Seven Lucky Gods|qq=|1=Koishikawa Shichifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463265}} * {{ill|Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|ja|下谷七福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|qq=|1=Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361333}} == See Also ==
* [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]]
== External Links ==
* [http://taitonavi.jp/pdf/pamph_ja/044.pdf Taitō Strolling Guide: Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage] {{Japan-culture-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|谷中七福神|version=87700998|comment=Imported full ja history}}

* '''[[:en:Kan'ei-ji|Kaneiji]]''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokokuin (Taitō)|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''[[:en:Tennō-ji (Taitō)|Tennōji (Taitō)]]''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanaka, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōanji (Taitō)|1=Chōan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106629524}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjōin (Arakawa)|1=Shusho-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97214954}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiunji (Arakawa)|1=Seiun-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97216427}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|1=Togaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019198}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiunji (Arakawa)|1=Keiunji (Arakawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418872}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: ** '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. ** '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. ** '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. ** '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.

[[Category:History of Taito, Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Kita, Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Arakawa Ward]] [[Category:Yanaka, Taito]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11632598}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:谷中七福神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11632598]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist pilgrimage | Name = Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods | image = | Location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Taitō|Taitō]], [[:en:Arakawa, Tokyo|Arakawa, Tokyo]], [[:en:北區|Kita, Tokyo]] | Distance = Approx. 5.5 km | Time = Approx. 2 hours | Period = January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 | Stamps = Paper or colored paper ¥200, Goshuin book ¥300 | Stamp Sheet = Paper sheet ¥1,000, unique to Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods (A3-sized Japanese paper) | Notes = Some temples have raised goshuin fees to ¥500 (as of recent years) }}
'''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (谷中七福神) is a '''pilgrimage route''' of seven '''Buddhist temples''' in '''Taitō, Arakawa, and Kita Wards, Tokyo''', each enshrining one of the [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]].
It is believed to have started '''about 250 years ago''', making it the '''oldest Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimage in Edo (Tokyo)'''. Unlike other Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimages, which include '''Shinto shrines''', the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods consist only of Buddhist temples'''.
During the pilgrimage period, visitors can purchase '''woodblock-printed stamp sheets''' to collect {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}} (temple stamps). Outside this period, although temple grounds are generally accessible, the '''Seven Lucky Gods statues may not be visible''', as '''halls may be closed'''.
== Overview ==
* '''Area:''' [[:en:Taitō|Taitō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Arakawa, Tokyo|Arakawa, Tokyo]], [[:en:北區|Kita, Tokyo]] * '''Distance:''' Approx. 5.5 km * '''Time required:''' Approx. 2 hours * '''Pilgrimage period:''' January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 * '''Temple stamps (Goshuin):''' ** '''Paper or colored paper:''' ¥200 ** '''Goshuin book (shuinchō):''' ¥300 ** '''Stamp sheet:''' ¥1,000, made of '''Japanese paper (not thick colored paper)''', unique to '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (approx. '''A3 size'''). ** In '''recent years''', some temples have increased the goshuin fee to '''¥500''' (others may follow suit). ** Although '''not officially announced''', '''if visitors bring a blank thick-paper sheet''', it is possible to collect '''goshuin''' on it (inquire at each temple).
== Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods List ==
* '''[[:en:Kan'ei-ji|Kaneiji]]''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokokuin (Taitō)|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''[[:en:Tennō-ji (Taitō)|Tennōji (Taitō)]]''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanaka, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōanji (Taitō)|1=Chōan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106629524}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjōin (Arakawa)|1=Shusho-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97214954}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiunji (Arakawa)|1=Seiun-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97216427}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|1=Togaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019198}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiunji (Arakawa)|1=Keiunji (Arakawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418872}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: ** '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. ** '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. ** '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. ** '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:谷中七福神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11632598]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist pilgrimage | Name = Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods | image = | Location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Taitō|Taitō]], [[:en:Arakawa, Tokyo|Arakawa, Tokyo]], [[:en:北區|Kita, Tokyo]] | Distance = Approx. 5.5 km | Time = Approx. 2 hours | Period = January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 | Stamps = Paper or colored paper ¥200, Goshuin book ¥300 | Stamp Sheet = Paper sheet ¥1,000, unique to Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods (A3-sized Japanese paper) | Notes = Some temples have raised goshuin fees to ¥500 (as of recent years) }}
'''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (谷中七福神) is a '''pilgrimage route''' of seven '''Buddhist temples''' in '''Taitō, Arakawa, and Kita Wards, Tokyo''', each enshrining one of the [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]].
It is believed to have started '''about 250 years ago''', making it the '''oldest Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimage in Edo (Tokyo)'''. Unlike other Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimages, which include '''Shinto shrines''', the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods consist only of Buddhist temples'''.
During the pilgrimage period, visitors can purchase '''woodblock-printed stamp sheets''' to collect {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}} (temple stamps). Outside this period, although temple grounds are generally accessible, the '''Seven Lucky Gods statues may not be visible''', as '''halls may be closed'''.
== Overview ==
* '''Area:''' [[:en:Taitō|Taitō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Arakawa, Tokyo|Arakawa, Tokyo]], [[:en:北區|Kita, Tokyo]] * '''Distance:''' Approx. 5.5 km * '''Time required:''' Approx. 2 hours * '''Pilgrimage period:''' January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 * '''Temple stamps (Goshuin):''' ** '''Paper or colored paper:''' ¥200 ** '''Goshuin book (shuinchō):''' ¥300 ** '''Stamp sheet:''' ¥1,000, made of '''Japanese paper (not thick colored paper)''', unique to '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (approx. '''A3 size'''). ** In '''recent years''', some temples have increased the goshuin fee to '''¥500''' (others may follow suit). ** Although '''not officially announced''', '''if visitors bring a blank thick-paper sheet''', it is possible to collect '''goshuin''' on it (inquire at each temple).
== Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods List ==
* '''[[:en:Kan'ei-ji|Kaneiji]]''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokokuin (Taitō)|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''[[:en:Tennō-ji (Taitō)|Tennōji (Taitō)]]''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanaka, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōanji (Taitō)|1=Chōan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106629524}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjōin (Arakawa)|1=Shusho-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97214954}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiunji (Arakawa)|1=Seiun-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97216427}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|1=Togaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019198}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiunji (Arakawa)|1=Keiunji (Arakawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418872}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: ** '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. ** '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. ** '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. ** '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.
== Nearby Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimages ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Seven Lucky Gods|ja|小石川七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koishikawa Seven Lucky Gods|1=Koishikawa Shichifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463265}} * {{ill|Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|ja|下谷七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|1=Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361333}} == See Also ==
* [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]]

== Overview ==
* '''Area:''' [[:en:Taitō|Taitō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Arakawa, Tokyo|Arakawa, Tokyo]], [[:en:北區|Kita, Tokyo]] * '''Distance:''' Approx. 5.5 km * '''Time required:''' Approx. 2 hours * '''Pilgrimage period:''' January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 * '''Temple stamps (Goshuin):''' ** '''Paper or colored paper:''' ¥200 ** '''Goshuin book (shuinchō):''' ¥300 ** '''Stamp sheet:''' ¥1,000, made of '''Japanese paper (not thick colored paper)''', unique to '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (approx. '''A3 size'''). ** In '''recent years''', some temples have increased the goshuin fee to '''¥500''' (others may follow suit). ** Although '''not officially announced''', '''if visitors bring a blank thick-paper sheet''', it is possible to collect '''goshuin''' on it (inquire at each temple).
== Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods List ==
* '''[[:en:Kan'ei-ji|Kaneiji]]''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokokuin (Taitō)|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''[[:en:Tennō-ji (Taitō)|Tennōji (Taitō)]]''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanaka, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōanji (Taitō)|1=Chōan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106629524}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjōin (Arakawa)|1=Shusho-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97214954}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiunji (Arakawa)|1=Seiun-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97216427}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|1=Togaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019198}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiunji (Arakawa)|1=Keiunji (Arakawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418872}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: ** '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. ** '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. ** '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. ** '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.

* [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:谷中七福神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11632598]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist pilgrimage | Name = Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods | image = | Location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Taitō|Taitō]], [[:en:Arakawa, Tokyo|Arakawa, Tokyo]], [[:en:北區|Kita, Tokyo]] | Distance = Approx. 5.5 km | Time = Approx. 2 hours | Period = January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 | Stamps = Paper or colored paper ¥200, Goshuin book ¥300 | Stamp Sheet = Paper sheet ¥1,000, unique to Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods (A3-sized Japanese paper) | Notes = Some temples have raised goshuin fees to ¥500 (as of recent years) }}
'''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (谷中七福神) is a '''pilgrimage route''' of seven '''Buddhist temples''' in '''Taitō, Arakawa, and Kita Wards, Tokyo''', each enshrining one of the [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]].
It is believed to have started '''about 250 years ago''', making it the '''oldest Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimage in Edo (Tokyo)'''. Unlike other Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimages, which include '''Shinto shrines''', the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods consist only of Buddhist temples'''.
During the pilgrimage period, visitors can purchase '''woodblock-printed stamp sheets''' to collect {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}} (temple stamps). Outside this period, although temple grounds are generally accessible, the '''Seven Lucky Gods statues may not be visible''', as '''halls may be closed'''.
== Overview ==
* '''Area:''' [[:en:Taitō|Taitō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Arakawa, Tokyo|Arakawa, Tokyo]], [[:en:北區|Kita, Tokyo]] * '''Distance:''' Approx. 5.5 km * '''Time required:''' Approx. 2 hours * '''Pilgrimage period:''' January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 * '''Temple stamps (Goshuin):''' ** '''Paper or colored paper:''' ¥200 ** '''Goshuin book (shuinchō):''' ¥300 ** '''Stamp sheet:''' ¥1,000, made of '''Japanese paper (not thick colored paper)''', unique to '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (approx. '''A3 size'''). ** In '''recent years''', some temples have increased the goshuin fee to '''¥500''' (others may follow suit). ** Although '''not officially announced''', '''if visitors bring a blank thick-paper sheet''', it is possible to collect '''goshuin''' on it (inquire at each temple).
== Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods List ==
* '''[[:en:Kan'ei-ji|Kaneiji]]''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokokuin (Taitō)|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''[[:en:Tennō-ji (Taitō)|Tennōji (Taitō)]]''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanaka, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōanji (Taitō)|1=Chōan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106629524}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjōin (Arakawa)|1=Shusho-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97214954}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiunji (Arakawa)|1=Seiun-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97216427}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|1=Togaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019198}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: ** '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. ** '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. ** '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. ** '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.

* '''[[:en:Kan'ei-ji|Kaneiji]]''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokokuin (Taitō)|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''[[:en:Tennō-ji (Taitō)|Tennōji (Taitō)]]''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanaka, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōanji (Taitō)|1=Chōan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106629524}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjōin (Arakawa)|1=Shusho-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97214954}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiunji (Arakawa)|1=Seiun-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97216427}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|1=Togaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019198}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiunji (Arakawa)|1=Keiunji (Arakawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418872}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: ** '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. ** '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. ** '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. ** '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.

* {{ill|Koishikawa Seven Lucky Gods|ja|小石川七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koishikawa Seven Lucky Gods|1=Koishikawa Shichifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463265}} * {{ill|Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|ja|下谷七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|1=Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361333}} == See Also ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:谷中七福神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11632598]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist pilgrimage | Name = Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods | image = | Location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Taitō|Taitō]], [[:en:Arakawa, Tokyo|Arakawa, Tokyo]], [[:en:北區|Kita, Tokyo]] | Distance = Approx. 5.5 km | Time = Approx. 2 hours | Period = January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 | Stamps = Paper or colored paper ¥200, Goshuin book ¥300 | Stamp Sheet = Paper sheet ¥1,000, unique to Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods (A3-sized Japanese paper) | Notes = Some temples have raised goshuin fees to ¥500 (as of recent years) }}
'''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (谷中七福神) is a '''pilgrimage route''' of seven '''Buddhist temples''' in '''Taitō, Arakawa, and Kita Wards, Tokyo''', each enshrining one of the [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]].
It is believed to have started '''about 250 years ago''', making it the '''oldest Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimage in Edo (Tokyo)'''. Unlike other Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimages, which include '''Shinto shrines''', the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods consist only of Buddhist temples'''.
During the pilgrimage period, visitors can purchase '''woodblock-printed stamp sheets''' to collect {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}} (temple stamps). Outside this period, although temple grounds are generally accessible, the '''Seven Lucky Gods statues may not be visible''', as '''halls may be closed'''.
== Overview ==
* '''Area:''' [[:en:Taitō|Taitō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Arakawa, Tokyo|Arakawa, Tokyo]], [[:en:北區|Kita, Tokyo]] * '''Distance:''' Approx. 5.5 km * '''Time required:''' Approx. 2 hours * '''Pilgrimage period:''' January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 * '''Temple stamps (Goshuin):''' ** '''Paper or colored paper:''' ¥200 ** '''Goshuin book (shuinchō):''' ¥300 ** '''Stamp sheet:''' ¥1,000, made of '''Japanese paper (not thick colored paper)''', unique to '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (approx. '''A3 size'''). ** In '''recent years''', some temples have increased the goshuin fee to '''¥500''' (others may follow suit). ** Although '''not officially announced''', '''if visitors bring a blank thick-paper sheet''', it is possible to collect '''goshuin''' on it (inquire at each temple).
== Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods List ==
* '''[[:en:Kan'ei-ji|Kaneiji]]''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokokuin (Taitō)|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''[[:en:Tennō-ji (Taitō)|Tennōji (Taitō)]]''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanaka, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōanji (Taitō)|1=Chōan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106629524}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjōin (Arakawa)|1=Shusho-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97214954}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiunji (Arakawa)|1=Seiun-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97216427}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|1=Togaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019198}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiunji (Arakawa)|1=Keiunji (Arakawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418872}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: ** '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. ** '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. ** '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. ** '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.

* '''[[:en:Kan'ei-ji|Kaneiji]]''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokokuin (Taitō)|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''[[:en:Tennō-ji (Taitō)|Tennōji (Taitō)]]''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanaka, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōanji (Taitō)|1=Chōan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106629524}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjōin (Arakawa)|1=Shusho-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97214954}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiunji (Arakawa)|1=Seiun-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97216427}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|1=Togaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019198}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiunji (Arakawa)|1=Keiunji (Arakawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418872}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''.
