<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:谷中七福神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11632598]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist pilgrimage | Name = Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods | image = | Location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Taitō|Taitō]], [[:en:Arakawa, Tokyo|Arakawa, Tokyo]], [[:en:北區|Kita, Tokyo]] | Distance = Approx. 5.5 km | Time = Approx. 2 hours | Period = January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 | Stamps = Paper or colored paper ¥200, Goshuin book ¥300 | Stamp Sheet = Paper sheet ¥1,000, unique to Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods (A3-sized Japanese paper) | Notes = Some temples have raised goshuin fees to ¥500 (as of recent years) }}
'''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (谷中七福神) is a '''pilgrimage route''' of seven '''Buddhist temples''' in '''Taitō, Arakawa, and Kita Wards, Tokyo''', each enshrining one of the [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]].
It is believed to have started '''about 250 years ago''', making it the '''oldest Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimage in Edo (Tokyo)'''. Unlike other Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimages, which include '''Shinto shrines''', the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods consist only of Buddhist temples'''.
During the pilgrimage period, visitors can purchase '''woodblock-printed stamp sheets''' to collect {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}} (temple stamps). Outside this period, although temple grounds are generally accessible, the '''Seven Lucky Gods statues may not be visible''', as '''halls may be closed'''.
== Overview ==
* '''Area:''' [[:en:Taitō|Taitō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Arakawa, Tokyo|Arakawa, Tokyo]], [[:en:北區|Kita, Tokyo]] * '''Distance:''' Approx. 5.5 km * '''Time required:''' Approx. 2 hours * '''Pilgrimage period:''' January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 * '''Temple stamps (Goshuin):''' ** '''Paper or colored paper:''' ¥200 ** '''Goshuin book (shuinchō):''' ¥300 ** '''Stamp sheet:''' ¥1,000, made of '''Japanese paper (not thick colored paper)''', unique to '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (approx. '''A3 size'''). ** In '''recent years''', some temples have increased the goshuin fee to '''¥500''' (others may follow suit). ** Although '''not officially announced''', '''if visitors bring a blank thick-paper sheet''', it is possible to collect '''goshuin''' on it (inquire at each temple).
== Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods List ==
* '''[[:en:Kan'ei-ji|Kaneiji]]''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokokuin (Taitō)|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''[[:en:Tennō-ji (Taitō)|Tennōji (Taitō)]]''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanaka, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōanji (Taitō)|1=Chōan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106629524}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjōin (Arakawa)|1=Shusho-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97214954}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiunji (Arakawa)|1=Seiun-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97216427}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|1=Togaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019198}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiunji (Arakawa)|1=Keiunji (Arakawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418872}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: ** '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. ** '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. ** '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. ** '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.

In the past: ** '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. ** '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. ** '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. ** '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:谷中七福神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:谷中七福神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:谷中七福神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11632598]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist pilgrimage | Name = Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods | image = | Location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Taitō|Taitō]], [[:en:Arakawa, Tokyo|Arakawa, Tokyo]], [[:en:北區|Kita, Tokyo]] | Distance = Approx. 5.5 km | Time = Approx. 2 hours | Period = January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 | Stamps = Paper or colored paper ¥200, Goshuin book ¥300 | Stamp Sheet = Paper sheet ¥1,000, unique to Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods (A3-sized Japanese paper) | Notes = Some temples have raised goshuin fees to ¥500 (as of recent years) }}
'''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (谷中七福神) is a '''pilgrimage route''' of seven '''Buddhist temples''' in '''Taitō, Arakawa, and Kita Wards, Tokyo''', each enshrining one of the [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]].
It is believed to have started '''about 250 years ago''', making it the '''oldest Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimage in Edo (Tokyo)'''. Unlike other Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimages, which include '''Shinto shrines''', the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods consist only of Buddhist temples'''.
During the pilgrimage period, visitors can purchase '''woodblock-printed stamp sheets''' to collect {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}} (temple stamps). Outside this period, although temple grounds are generally accessible, the '''Seven Lucky Gods statues may not be visible''', as '''halls may be closed'''.
== Overview ==
* '''Area:''' [[:en:Taitō|Taitō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Arakawa, Tokyo|Arakawa, Tokyo]], [[:en:北區|Kita, Tokyo]] * '''Distance:''' Approx. 5.5 km * '''Time required:''' Approx. 2 hours * '''Pilgrimage period:''' January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 * '''Temple stamps (Goshuin):''' ** '''Paper or colored paper:''' ¥200 ** '''Goshuin book (shuinchō):''' ¥300 ** '''Stamp sheet:''' ¥1,000, made of '''Japanese paper (not thick colored paper)''', unique to '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (approx. '''A3 size'''). ** In '''recent years''', some temples have increased the goshuin fee to '''¥500''' (others may follow suit). ** Although '''not officially announced''', '''if visitors bring a blank thick-paper sheet''', it is possible to collect '''goshuin''' on it (inquire at each temple).
== Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods List ==
* '''[[:en:Kan'ei-ji|Kaneiji]]''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokokuin (Taitō)|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''[[:en:Tennō-ji (Taitō)|Tennōji (Taitō)]]''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanaka, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōanji (Taitō)|1=Chōan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106629524}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjōin (Arakawa)|1=Shusho-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97214954}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiunji (Arakawa)|1=Seiun-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97216427}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|1=Togaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019198}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiunji (Arakawa)|1=Keiunji (Arakawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418872}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: ** '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. ** '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. ** '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. ** '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.
== Nearby Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimages ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Seven Lucky Gods|ja|小石川七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koishikawa Seven Lucky Gods|1=Koishikawa Shichifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463265}} * {{ill|Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|ja|下谷七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|1=Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361333}} == See Also ==
* [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]]
== External Links ==

It is believed to have started '''about 250 years ago''', making it the '''oldest Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimage in Edo (Tokyo)'''. Unlike other Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimages, which include '''Shinto shrines''', the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods consist only of Buddhist temples'''.

'''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (谷中七福神) is a '''pilgrimage route''' of seven '''Buddhist temples''' in '''Taitō, Arakawa, and Kita Wards, Tokyo''', each enshrining one of the [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]].
It is believed to have started '''about 250 years ago''', making it the '''oldest Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimage in Edo (Tokyo)'''. Unlike other Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimages, which include '''Shinto shrines''', the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods consist only of Buddhist temples'''.
During the pilgrimage period, visitors can purchase '''woodblock-printed stamp sheets''' to collect {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}} (temple stamps). Outside this period, although temple grounds are generally accessible, the '''Seven Lucky Gods statues may not be visible''', as '''halls may be closed'''.
== Overview ==
* '''Area:''' [[:en:Taitō|Taitō, Tokyo]], [[:en:Arakawa, Tokyo|Arakawa, Tokyo]], [[:en:北區|Kita, Tokyo]] * '''Distance:''' Approx. 5.5 km * '''Time required:''' Approx. 2 hours * '''Pilgrimage period:''' January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 * '''Temple stamps (Goshuin):''' ** '''Paper or colored paper:''' ¥200 ** '''Goshuin book (shuinchō):''' ¥300 ** '''Stamp sheet:''' ¥1,000, made of '''Japanese paper (not thick colored paper)''', unique to '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (approx. '''A3 size'''). ** In '''recent years''', some temples have increased the goshuin fee to '''¥500''' (others may follow suit). ** Although '''not officially announced''', '''if visitors bring a blank thick-paper sheet''', it is possible to collect '''goshuin''' on it (inquire at each temple).
== Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods List ==
* '''[[:en:Kan'ei-ji|Kaneiji]]''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokokuin (Taitō)|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''[[:en:Tennō-ji (Taitō)|Tennōji (Taitō)]]''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanaka, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōanji (Taitō)|1=Chōan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106629524}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjōin (Arakawa)|1=Shusho-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97214954}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiunji (Arakawa)|1=Seiun-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97216427}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|1=Togaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019198}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiunji (Arakawa)|1=Keiunji (Arakawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418872}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: ** '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. ** '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. ** '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. ** '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.
== Nearby Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimages ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Seven Lucky Gods|ja|小石川七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koishikawa Seven Lucky Gods|1=Koishikawa Shichifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463265}} * {{ill|Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|ja|下谷七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|1=Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361333}} == See Also ==
* [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]]
== External Links ==
* [http://taitonavi.jp/pdf/pamph_ja/044.pdf Taitō Strolling Guide: Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage] {{Japan-culture-stub}}

'''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (谷中七福神) is a '''pilgrimage route''' of seven '''Buddhist temples''' in '''Taitō, Arakawa, and Kita Wards, Tokyo''', each enshrining one of the [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]].
It is believed to have started '''about 250 years ago''', making it the '''oldest Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimage in Edo (Tokyo)'''. Unlike other Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimages, which include '''Shinto shrines''', the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods consist only of Buddhist temples'''.
During the pilgrimage period, visitors can purchase '''woodblock-printed stamp sheets''' to collect {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}} (temple stamps). Outside this period, although temple grounds are generally accessible, the '''Seven Lucky Gods statues may not be visible''', as '''halls may be closed'''.

'''A3 size'''). ** In '''recent years''', some temples have increased the goshuin fee to '''¥500''' (others may follow suit). ** Although '''not officially announced''', '''if visitors bring a blank thick-paper sheet''', it is possible to collect '''goshuin''' on it (inquire at each temple).
== Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods List ==
* '''{{ill|Kan'ei-ji|en|Kan'ei-ji|WD=Q3120766|lt=Kaneiji}}''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokokuin (Taitō)|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Tennō-ji (Taitō)|en|Tennō-ji (Taitō)|WD=Q17218754|lt=Tennōji (Taitō)}}''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanaka, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōanji (Taitō)|1=Chōan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106629524}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjōin (Arakawa)|1=Shusho-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97214954}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiunji (Arakawa)|1=Seiun-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97216427}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|1=Togaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019198}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiunji (Arakawa)|1=Keiunji (Arakawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418872}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: ** '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. ** '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. ** '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. ** '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:谷中七福神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:谷中七福神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:谷中七福神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11632598]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist pilgrimage | Name = Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods | image = | Location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|WD=Q232641|lt=Taitō}}, {{ill|Arakawa, Tokyo|en|Arakawa, Tokyo|WD=Q232624|lt=Arakawa, Tokyo}}, {{ill|北區|en|北區|WD=Q1180096|lt=Kita, Tokyo}} | Distance = Approx. 5.5 km | Time = Approx. 2 hours | Period = January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 | Stamps = Paper or colored paper ¥200, Goshuin book ¥300 | Stamp Sheet = Paper sheet ¥1,000, unique to Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods (A3-sized Japanese paper) | Notes = Some temples have raised goshuin fees to ¥500 (as of recent years) }}
'''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (谷中七福神) is a '''pilgrimage route''' of seven '''Buddhist temples''' in '''Taitō, Arakawa, and Kita Wards, Tokyo''', each enshrining one of the {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|WD=Q846858|lt=Seven Lucky Gods}}.
It is believed to have started '''about 250 years ago''', making it the '''oldest Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimage in Edo (Tokyo)'''. Unlike other Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimages, which include '''Shinto shrines''', the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods consist only of Buddhist temples'''.
During the pilgrimage period, visitors can purchase '''woodblock-printed stamp sheets''' to collect {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}} (temple stamps). Outside this period, although temple grounds are generally accessible, the '''Seven Lucky Gods statues may not be visible''', as '''halls may be closed'''.
== Overview ==
* '''Area:''' {{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|WD=Q232641|lt=Taitō, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Arakawa, Tokyo|en|Arakawa, Tokyo|WD=Q232624|lt=Arakawa, Tokyo}}, {{ill|北區|en|北區|WD=Q1180096|lt=Kita, Tokyo}} * '''Distance:''' Approx. 5.5 km * '''Time required:''' Approx. 2 hours * '''Pilgrimage period:''' January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 * '''Temple stamps (Goshuin):''' ** '''Paper or colored paper:''' ¥200 ** '''Goshuin book (shuinchō):''' ¥300 ** '''Stamp sheet:''' ¥1,000, made of '''Japanese paper (not thick colored paper)''', unique to '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (approx. '''A3 size'''). ** In '''recent years''', some temples have increased the goshuin fee to '''¥500''' (others may follow suit). ** Although '''not officially announced''', '''if visitors bring a blank thick-paper sheet''', it is possible to collect '''goshuin''' on it (inquire at each temple).
== Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods List ==
* '''{{ill|Kan'ei-ji|en|Kan'ei-ji|WD=Q3120766|lt=Kaneiji}}''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokokuin (Taitō)|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Tennō-ji (Taitō)|en|Tennō-ji (Taitō)|WD=Q17218754|lt=Tennōji (Taitō)}}''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanaka, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōanji (Taitō)|1=Chōan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106629524}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjōin (Arakawa)|1=Shusho-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97214954}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiunji (Arakawa)|1=Seiun-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97216427}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|1=Togaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019198}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiunji (Arakawa)|1=Keiunji (Arakawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418872}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: ** '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. ** '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. ** '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. ** '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.
== Nearby Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimages ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Seven Lucky Gods|ja|小石川七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koishikawa Seven Lucky Gods|1=Koishikawa Shichifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463265}} * {{ill|Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|ja|下谷七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|1=Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361333}} == See Also ==

'''A3 size'''). ** In '''recent years''', some temples have increased the goshuin fee to '''¥500''' (others may follow suit). ** Although '''not officially announced''', '''if visitors bring a blank thick-paper sheet''', it is possible to collect '''goshuin''' on it (inquire at each temple).

* '''{{ill|Kan'ei-ji|en|Kan'ei-ji|WD=Q3120766|lt=Kaneiji}}''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokokuin (Taitō)|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Tennō-ji (Taitō)|en|Tennō-ji (Taitō)|WD=Q17218754|lt=Tennōji (Taitō)}}''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanaka, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōanji (Taitō)|1=Chōan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106629524}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjōin (Arakawa)|1=Shusho-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97214954}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiunji (Arakawa)|1=Seiun-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97216427}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|1=Togaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019198}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiunji (Arakawa)|1=Keiunji (Arakawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418872}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: ** '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. ** '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. ** '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. ** '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:谷中七福神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:谷中七福神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:谷中七福神]]
{{Infobox Buddhist pilgrimage | Name = Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods | image = | Location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|WD=Q232641|lt=Taitō}}, {{ill|Arakawa, Tokyo|en|Arakawa, Tokyo|WD=Q232624|lt=Arakawa, Tokyo}}, {{ill|北區|en|北區|WD=Q1180096|lt=Kita, Tokyo}} | Distance = Approx. 5.5 km | Time = Approx. 2 hours | Period = January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 | Stamps = Paper or colored paper ¥200, Goshuin book ¥300 | Stamp Sheet = Paper sheet ¥1,000, unique to Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods (A3-sized Japanese paper) | Notes = Some temples have raised goshuin fees to ¥500 (as of recent years) }}
'''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (谷中七福神) is a '''pilgrimage route''' of seven '''Buddhist temples''' in '''Taitō, Arakawa, and Kita Wards, Tokyo''', each enshrining one of the {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|WD=Q846858|lt=Seven Lucky Gods}}.
It is believed to have started '''about 250 years ago''', making it the '''oldest Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimage in Edo (Tokyo)'''. Unlike other Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimages, which include '''Shinto shrines''', the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods consist only of Buddhist temples'''.
During the pilgrimage period, visitors can purchase '''woodblock-printed stamp sheets''' to collect {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}} (temple stamps). Outside this period, although temple grounds are generally accessible, the '''Seven Lucky Gods statues may not be visible''', as '''halls may be closed'''.
== Overview ==
* '''Area:''' {{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|WD=Q232641|lt=Taitō, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Arakawa, Tokyo|en|Arakawa, Tokyo|WD=Q232624|lt=Arakawa, Tokyo}}, {{ill|北區|en|北區|WD=Q1180096|lt=Kita, Tokyo}} * '''Distance:''' Approx. 5.5 km * '''Time required:''' Approx. 2 hours * '''Pilgrimage period:''' January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 * '''Temple stamps (Goshuin):''' ** '''Paper or colored paper:''' ¥200 ** '''Goshuin book (shuinchō):''' ¥300 ** '''Stamp sheet:''' ¥1,000, made of '''Japanese paper (not thick colored paper)''', unique to '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (approx. '''A3 size'''). ** In '''recent years''', some temples have increased the goshuin fee to '''¥500''' (others may follow suit). ** Although '''not officially announced''', '''if visitors bring a blank thick-paper sheet''', it is possible to collect '''goshuin''' on it (inquire at each temple).
== Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods List ==
* '''{{ill|Kan'ei-ji|en|Kan'ei-ji|WD=Q3120766|lt=Kaneiji}}''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokokuin (Taitō)|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Tennō-ji (Taitō)|en|Tennō-ji (Taitō)|WD=Q17218754|lt=Tennōji (Taitō)}}''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanaka, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōanji (Taitō)|1=Chōan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106629524}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjōin (Arakawa)|1=Shusho-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97214954}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiunji (Arakawa)|1=Seiun-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97216427}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|1=Togaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019198}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiunji (Arakawa)|1=Keiunji (Arakawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418872}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: ** '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. ** '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. ** '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. ** '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.
== Nearby Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimages ==
* {{ill|Koishikawa Seven Lucky Gods|ja|小石川七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koishikawa Seven Lucky Gods|1=Koishikawa Shichifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463265}} * {{ill|Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|ja|下谷七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|1=Shitaya Seven Lucky Gods|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361333}} == See Also ==
* {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|WD=Q846858|lt=Seven Lucky Gods}}
== External Links ==

* '''{{ill|Kan'ei-ji|en|Kan'ei-ji|WD=Q3120766|lt=Kaneiji|1=Kan'ei-ji}}''' – '''Benzaiten''' (弁才天) at '''Benten-dō''' (不忍池弁天堂) – {{ill|Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja|上野桜木, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenosakuragi, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Gokokuin (Taitō)|ja|護国院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokokuin (Taitō)|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}}''' (sub-temple of Kaneiji) – '''Daikokuten''' (大黒天) – {{ill|Ueno Park|ja|上野恩賜公園, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Tennō-ji (Taitō)|en|Tennō-ji (Taitō)|WD=Q17218754|lt=Tennōji (Taitō)|1=Tennō-ji (Taitō)}}''' – '''Bishamonten''' (毘沙門天) – {{ill|Yanaka, Taitō|ja|谷中, 台東区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanaka, Taitō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Chōanji (Taitō)|ja|長安寺 (台東区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōanji (Taitō)|1=Chōan-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106629524}}''' – '''Jurojin''' (寿老人) – Yanaka, Taitō * '''{{ill|Shōjōin (Arakawa)|ja|修性院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjōin (Arakawa)|1=Shusho-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97214954}}''' – '''Hotei''' (布袋) – {{ill|Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja|西日暮里, 荒川区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinippori, Arakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''{{ill|Seiunji (Arakawa)|ja|青雲寺 (荒川区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiunji (Arakawa)|1=Seiun-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97216427}}''' – '''Ebisu''' (恵比須) – Nishinippori, Arakawa * '''{{ill|Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|ja|東覚寺 (東京都北区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkakuji (Tokyo)|1=Togaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019198}}''' – '''Fukurokuju''' (福禄寿) – {{ill|Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja|田端, 北区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabata, Kita, Tokyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Near '''Shōjōin''', there is '''{{ill|Keiunji (Arakawa)|ja|啓運寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiunji (Arakawa)|1=Q11418872|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418872}}''', which enshrines '''Bishamonten'''. Many pilgrims '''pass by this temple''', but it is '''not part of the official Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods route'''. * The composition of the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' was '''not fixed during the Edo period'''. In the past: ** '''Ebisu''' was enshrined at '''Kanda Myōjin''' or '''Shimizu Kannon-dō'''. ** '''Daikokuten''' was at '''Kyōōji'''. ** '''Bishamonten''' was at '''Hon-dōji in Yanaka'''. ** '''Benzaiten''' was at '''Yōsenji in Kitamachi'''.

{{Infobox Buddhist pilgrimage | Name = Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods | image = | Location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|WD=Q232641|lt=Taitō|1=Taitō}}, {{ill|Arakawa, Tokyo|en|Arakawa, Tokyo|WD=Q232624|lt=Arakawa, Tokyo|1=Arakawa, Tokyo}}, {{ill|北區|en|北區|WD=Q1180096|lt=Kita, Tokyo|1=北區}} | Distance = Approx. 5.5 km | Time = Approx. 2 hours | Period = January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 | Stamps = Paper or colored paper ¥200, Goshuin book ¥300 | Stamp Sheet = Paper sheet ¥1,000, unique to Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods (A3-sized Japanese paper) | Notes = Some temples have raised goshuin fees to ¥500 (as of recent years) }}
'''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (谷中七福神) is a '''pilgrimage route''' of seven '''Buddhist temples''' in '''Taitō, Arakawa, and Kita Wards, Tokyo''', each enshrining one of the {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|WD=Q846858|lt=Seven Lucky Gods|1=Seven Lucky Gods}}.
It is believed to have started '''about 250 years ago''', making it the '''oldest Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimage in Edo (Tokyo)'''. Unlike other Seven Lucky Gods pilgrimages, which include '''Shinto shrines''', the '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods consist only of Buddhist temples'''.
During the pilgrimage period, visitors can purchase '''woodblock-printed stamp sheets''' to collect {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}} (temple stamps). Outside this period, although temple grounds are generally accessible, the '''Seven Lucky Gods statues may not be visible''', as '''halls may be closed'''.
== Overview ==
* '''Area:''' {{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|WD=Q232641|lt=Taitō, Tokyo|1=Taitō}}, {{ill|Arakawa, Tokyo|en|Arakawa, Tokyo|WD=Q232624|lt=Arakawa, Tokyo|1=Arakawa, Tokyo}}, {{ill|北區|en|北區|WD=Q1180096|lt=Kita, Tokyo|1=北區}} * '''Distance:''' Approx. 5.5 km * '''Time required:''' Approx. 2 hours * '''Pilgrimage period:''' January 1 – January 10 (until 2009, January 1 – January 15) / 9:00 – 17:00 * '''Temple stamps (Goshuin):''' ** '''Paper or colored paper:''' ¥200 ** '''Goshuin book (shuinchō):''' ¥300 ** '''Stamp sheet:''' ¥1,000, made of '''Japanese paper (not thick colored paper)''', unique to '''Yanaka Seven Lucky Gods''' (approx. '''A3 size'''). ** In '''recent years''', some temples have increased the goshuin fee to '''¥500''' (others may follow suit). ** Although '''not officially announced''', '''if visitors bring a blank thick-paper sheet''', it is possible to collect '''goshuin''' on it (inquire at each temple).
